title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · History Brief: Gita Index Conflict Stages
summary: A repeated long-running integration conflicts across generated code, renamed policy files, and tests; previous manual resolutions are inconsistent and one clean textual result changes behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita index conflict stages increment in three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Index Conflict Stages
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G84 is a theory in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Index Conflict Stages contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Index Conflict Stages while producing three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G84 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing. A defensible solution applies Gita Index Conflict Stages, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-index-conflict-stages — Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Index Conflict Stages evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-index-conflict-stages-m2uzws" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Index Conflict Stages into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-index-conflict-stages-m2uzws
# scenario-key: trace-m2uzws probe-swzu2m boundary-uzwsm2 owner-m2uzwsx fault-m2uzwsz
# before-ref: main=a11cem2 candidate=b00b1ws
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11cem2 candidate=c0ffees
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
